raw_consumer_statement: I logged into my online acct to pay a monthly charge to my card ( this is the only purchase I used the card for ). I discovered over {$5000.00} in fraudulent charges to my card. I contacted Barclays. The customer service rep informed me that she would refer the issue to their fraud department to open an investigation. She also told me that she showed in my acct notes that an individual ( who was not me! ) called and updated my home address. She also told me that shortly after that an individual called to report a lost or stolen card and requested a new card be sent. That new card was sent to the fraudulent address. During the phone call I created a verbal password. I also updated my address, locked my card, and went online to freeze my credit at the 3 credit bureaus. Barclays removed the fraudulent charges. But then informed me that because a chip was present I would be held responsible for the charges. I called again. I went through the same process. Barclay removed the charges again. And then they once again sent me a letter telling me that I would be responsible. I called again. And the same thing happened. The last time the majority of the charges were removed. There are currently almost {$600.00} still on the card that they have not removed. I called again. The rep told me that they should be removed soon ( I was told working 30 days of XX/XX/year> ). This has still not happened. In the meantime time Barclays has added late fees for nonpayment, interest charges, and reported nonpayment to the credit bureaus. Now my credit rating is falling and they still have not corrected the issue! This has been going on for five months!!!
